summary: Matched and typed the baselib hash search path by adding concrete HSD_Hash/HSD_HashEntry structures, implementing HSD_HashSearch in hash.c, and replacing an anonymous class.c global with a typed HSD_Hash* current_hash. The PR had no discussion or review comments, so the rationale is inferred from the diff: the main value was improving type accuracy around class-library hash lookup while preserving the matched behavior of hsdSearchClassInfo.
what_changed: Implemented HSD_HashSearch(HSD_Hash* hashp, void* key, int* success) in src/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.c.

HSD_HashSearch now obtains an index through hashp->parent.class_info->getidx(hashp), asserts idx < hashp->table_size with HSD_ASSERT(113, ...), calls HashSearchEntry(hashp, idx, key, NULL), optionally writes a boolean success value, and returns entry->value cast to HSD_ClassInfo* or NULL.

Expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/hash.h from an untyped HSD_HashSearch(u32, const char*, int) declaration to concrete HSD_HashEntry, HSD_HashClass, HSD_HashClassInfo, and HSD_Hash definitions plus typed prototypes for HashSearchEntry and HSD_HashSearch.
Added forward declarations for HSD_Hash and HSD_HashEntry in src/sysdolphin/baselib/forward.h.
Renamed and retyped the static class.c global HSD_Class_804D7708 from u32 to HSD_Hash* current_hash.
Updated _hsdClassAmnesia and hsdForgetClassLibrary to reset current_hash to NULL instead of assigning 0 to the old u32 global.
Updated hsdSearchClassInfo to call HSD_HashSearch(current_hash, (void*) class_name, 0) only when current_hash is non-NULL.
smart_moves: Recovered the anonymous HSD_Class_804D7708 global as a pointer to HSD_Hash, which made downstream code self-documenting and enabled a strongly typed HSD_HashSearch signature.

Moved hash internals into hash.h only as far as needed for matching and call-site typing: HSD_Hash contains parent, table, and table_size, while HSD_HashClassInfo exposes the getidx callback needed by HSD_HashSearch.
Preserved the original call behavior from hsdSearchClassInfo by passing a NULL/0 success pointer while adapting the key argument to the new generic void* API.
Used NULL for pointer state in class.c, improving semantic clarity over integer 0 assignments.
Kept the HSD_HashSearch implementation compact and close to the observed control flow: compute index, assert bounds, search entry, set success, return value or NULL.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown static u32 is only used as an opaque handle to a subsystem, check whether it should be a typed pointer; here HSD_Class_804D7708 became HSD_Hash* current_hash.
Matching can require changing a public prototype from guessed scalar/string parameters to the real object-oriented baselib pattern: object pointer plus generic key plus optional success out-param.
For baselib class-like structs, preserve the parent/class_info layout so method dispatch through function pointers, such as class_info->getidx, can be represented accurately.
Optional out-parameters should be typed as pointers and guarded before assignment; the implementation writes *success = !!entry only when success is non-NULL.
Header work is often part of matching: adding forward declarations and struct definitions can unblock more accurate function bodies without changing behavior.
When a hash lookup stores generic values, returning a specific type may still require a cast at the boundary; HSD_HashSearch returns entry->value as HSD_ClassInfo*.
naming_conventions: Recovered globals should use role-based names when evidence supports them: HSD_Class_804D7708 was renamed to current_hash.
Hash data structures use HSD_ prefixes: HSD_Hash, HSD_HashEntry, HSD_HashClass, HSD_HashClassInfo.
Hash entry fields are conventional linked-list/key-value names: next, key, value.
Hash object fields follow observed structure roles: parent, table, table_size.
Function names retain baselib/HSD capitalization style: HSD_HashSearch and HashSearchEntry.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use a typed object pointer for the first parameter when code dereferences nested fields like hashp->parent.class_info->getidx(hashp).
Represent class-method calls through nested class_info function pointers rather than flattening them into helper calls or integer arithmetic.
Match boolean success writes with double-negation, *success = !!entry, when the original code likely stores 0/1 from a pointer test.
Use the exact assertion site number from the matched function, HSD_ASSERT(113, idx < hashp->table_size).
Pass NULL to HashSearchEntry's fourth parameter from HSD_HashSearch, preserving the observed unused/out-link behavior.
Keep hsdSearchClassInfo's success argument as 0/NULL to avoid introducing an unnecessary local variable.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that implement or type additional hash helpers such as HashSearchEntry.
Search for remaining HSD_Class_804D-style anonymous globals that may be typed baselib object pointers.
Check whether HSD_HashClass/HSD_HashClassInfo naming or layout was refined in subsequent baselib header cleanup PRs.
Search for other call sites of HSD_HashSearch to see whether success out-parameter usage was later matched.
